what_it_does: Defines an SVG line icon of a square window-like frame divided into four panes by one vertical and one horizontal line.
observations: The SVG uses a 24 by 24 viewBox.
The icon has no fill and uses currentColor for its stroke.
Stroke width is 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
The outer shape is a 16 by 16 rectangle positioned at x=4 and y=4 with rx=1.
A single path draws the center vertical and horizontal dividers.
standouts: Two SVG primitives create the full icon: one rectangle and one divider path.
conventions: Uses common inline SVG icon attributes for scalable line icons.
Keeps styling on the root SVG element rather than per-shape overrides.
concerns: No title, role, aria-label, or aria-hidden metadata is present in the SVG itself.
notes: A minimal themeable window icon made from an outer frame and crossed pane dividers.
questions: Should this SVG be decorative or exposed with an accessible label when rendered?
Is the slight corner radius intentional for this icon family?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a window icon, and the SVG content matches that by drawing a divided window frame.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable interface icon for a window, layout pane, or divided frame concept.
contribution: Contributes a compact, scalable, color-inheriting window/pane icon that can be retained as a neutral visual substrate in a fused design system.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 SVG line icon showing a square window frame divided into four panes with currentColor stroke styling.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a window or pane-grid concept as a reusable design-system icon asset.
